<START> Play slower games, review your mistakes, solve tactical puzzles regularly, and try not to leave pieces undefended. Those habits can make a big difference. <END>

<START> Try a puzzle that focuses on forks or pins. They're common tactical ideas and excellent for improving calculation. <END>

<START> If you're new to chess, the Italian Game is a solid choice. It teaches development, king safety, and central control without too much theory. <END>
